portfolio.info_activity/group_description_act/desc:
Strategic goals/objectives 
of the project: Construction and supervision of Base-Gicumbi-Rukomo Road (51.54 km) 

General achievement of 
strategic goals /main 
component of the 
projects:
1. Upgrading of 51.54 km between Base and Rukomo, including a 
component for the sensitization of local communities on sexually 
transmitted diseases, environmental protection and road safety;
2. Related improvements comprising the construction of 
multifunctional centres and rehabilitation of school facilities along 
the main road;
3. Institutional support to the project executing agency, and 
4. Clearing the right-of-way.

Specific achievement of 
strategic goals/outputs of 
the projects:
1. Improvement of road quality riding,
2. Reduction of transport cost

The project’s sector goal is to contribute to the opening up of the country’s interior and promote trade between northern and eastern parts of Rwanda through a direct link between the towns of Rubavu, Musanze, Gicumbi and Nyagatare.
The Base-Gicumbi-Rukomo section is part of the 125 km Base-Nyagatare road on the northern corridor connecting Rwanda to the Port of Mombasa in 
Kenya and is one of the priority roads retained under the East African Community (EAC) Transport Strategy.

Other important 
background information 
of the project:
The Transport Sector Support Project aims to improve accessibility and mobility between the towns of Base, Gicumbi, Rukomo and Nyagatare and the living conditions of the population within its zone of influence.
portfolio.info_activity/group_description_act/expe:
The following assumptions have been made during the planning process for the project:
(i) Improvement of road quality riding
(ii) Reduction of transport cost
(iii) Increase of average annually daily traffic 
(iv) Increase of agricultural activities
portfolio.info_activity/group_description_act/obta:
(i) Improvement of road quality riding (road works completed)
(ii) Reduction of transport cost (travel time reduced from 4 hours to 1 hours)
(iii) Increase of average annually daily traffic (boom of diverted traffic)
(iv) Increase of agricultural activities: remarkable.
